title: U.S. Treasury Department Releases Report on the Historically Equitable Economic Recovery From the Pandemic
summary: The Treasury Department released a report on October 23, 2023, asserting that the COVID-19 economic recovery was the most equitable in recent history, with Black and Hispanic families experiencing faster unemployment recovery, larger wealth gains (60% median wealth increase for Black families from 2019-2022), and significant gains in homeownership and business ownership relative to past recessions.
key_points: Published October 23, 2023 — significantly historical relative to TODAY (2026-04-10), over 29 months old
From 2019 to 2022, median inflation-adjusted wealth rose 60% for Black families, 47% for Hispanic families, and 31% for white families
Black and Hispanic unemployment rates recovered to near historic lows within 20 months of the COVID recession peak
Real earnings growth over the recovery period was 4.0% for Black workers and 2.4% for Hispanic workers (vs. declines after the Great Recession)
Black and Hispanic homeownership rates rose 2.9 and 1.2 percentage points respectively from 2019-2022 despite rising interest rates
Black and Hispanic business ownership rates reached 11% and 9.8% respectively, the highest recorded readings in Survey of Consumer Finances history
why_it_matters: This source is a retrospective policy advocacy document from the Treasury Department summarizing pandemic-era recovery statistics and attributing outcomes to the American Rescue Plan. It contains no forward-looking economic signals, policy decisions, or market-moving content. It is not relevant to macro catalysts or market structure.
